name: Log clinic chatbot inquiries from website widget to Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## AI Clinic Receptionist Chatbot with Google Sheets Logging

### Who is this for?
Clinics, solo doctors, and healthcare businesses that want a 24/7 virtual receptionist embedded on their website without hiring extra staff.

### What it does
This workflow powers an AI chat widget on a clinic website. Patients can send inquiries any time of day and get instant, context-aware replies from an AI receptionist named Medi.

As the conversation progresses, the AI silently extracts key details such as name, contact, service interest, appointment date and time, doctor preference, and reason for visit, and logs everything to a Google Sheet automatically. One row per visitor, updated in real time. No manual data entry needed.

### How it works
1. The chat widget on the website sends a POST request to the webhook with the patient's message and a session ID
2. The AI Agent (Medi) responds using clinic knowledge baked into its system prompt
3. Conversation memory keeps context across the full session
4. A hidden data block in every AI reply is stripped before display and parsed for structured info
5. If any patient data was collected, the Google Sheet row is created or updated
6. The clean reply is returned to the chat widget

### Requirements
- OpenAI API key (gpt-4o-mini)
- Google Sheets OAuth2 connection
- A clinic website to embed the chat widget on

### How to customize
- Update the AI system prompt with your real clinic name, services, doctors, and contact info
- Adjust the sheet column names to match your own Google Sheet
- Change the session memory window size depending on how long your typical conversations run

## Setup Instructions

**Step 1 — Connect OpenAI**
Open the OpenAI Chat Model node and connect your OpenAI API key credential.

**Step 2 — Connect Google Sheets**
Open the Update Inquirer Row in Sheets node and connect your Google account via OAuth2.

**Step 3 — Set your Sheet ID**
Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID in the Google Sheets node with the ID from your sheet URL:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/YOUR_ID_HERE/edit

**Step 4 — Prepare your Google Sheet**
Create a tab named Inquiries with these exact headers in Row 1:

Session ID | Last Updated | Name | Contact | Intent | Service Interested | Appointment Date | Appointment Time | Doctor Preference | Reason for Visit | Conversation Summary | Status

**Step 5 — Update the AI system prompt**
Open the Medi - AI Receptionist Agent node and update the system prompt with your real clinic name, address, phone, email, services, and doctors.

**Step 6 — Activate and grab the webhook URL**
Activate the workflow. Copy the webhook URL from the Receive Chat Message node and paste it into your chatbot.js file as the N8N_WEBHOOK_URL value.

## Step 1 — Receive Message

Accepts POST requests from the chat widget. Each request includes:
- `message` — the patient's text
- `sessionId` — unique browser ID
- `timestamp` — ISO datetime

Response mode is set to Response Node so the reply waits for the full AI output before returning.

## Step 2 — AI Agent (Medi)

Runs the AI receptionist with full clinic knowledge in the system prompt. The Session Memory node uses the visitor's sessionId as the key, storing the last 20 messages so context is never lost mid-conversation.

At the end of every reply, the AI appends a hidden [INQUIRER_DATA] JSON block with all known patient details. This block is invisible to the patient — it gets stripped in the next step.

## Step 3 — Parse & Strip

The Code node does two things:

1. Removes the [INQUIRER_DATA] block from the reply so the patient never sees raw JSON in the chat

2. Parses the block and extracts all structured fields into separate output values

Sets `hasData = true` if at least one meaningful field (name, contact, or intent) was collected.

## Step 4 — Conditional Log

Skips writing to Google Sheets on early turns before the patient has shared any details. Only logs when hasData is true.

TRUE path: write to Sheets, then reply
FALSE path: skip Sheets, reply immediately

This avoids creating empty rows for every single message exchange.

## Step 5 — Log to Google Sheets

Uses appendOrUpdate matched on Session ID. Each visitor gets exactly one row, updated in real time as more details are collected during the conversation.

Status values:
- `new` — just started
- `in_progress` — chatting
- `appointment_set` — date and time confirmed
- `completed` — inquiry resolved
- `needs_followup` — requires staff action

## Step 6 — Reply to Widget

Sends the cleaned reply back to the chat widget as:
`{ "reply": "..." }`

Includes CORS headers so the widget can call this from any domain. The chatbot.js widget expects this exact response shape.

options.systemMessage:
You are Medi, the friendly and professional virtual receptionist for MediCare Clinic located at 123 Health Street, Cebu City, Philippines.

Your job is to assist patients and visitors with their inquiries. Always be warm, helpful, and concise. Use simple language.

Today's date is {{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd (EEEE)') }}. Use it to resolve relative dates like "tomorrow" or "next Monday".

## CLINIC INFORMATION
- Name: MediCare Clinic
- Address: 123 Health Street, Cebu City, Philippines
- Phone: +1234567890
- Email: user@example.com
- Hours: Monday–Saturday 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sunday 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM

## SERVICES OFFERED
- General Consultation
- Pediatrics
- Women's Health
- Laboratory Services
- Pharmacy

## DOCTORS
- Dr. Maria Santos — General Medicine
- Dr. Jose Reyes — Pediatrics
- Dr. Ana Lim — Women's Health

## HOW TO BOOK AN APPOINTMENT
1. Call us at +1234567890. Email user@example.com
3. Walk in during clinic hours

## COLLECTING INFORMATION
During the conversation, naturally and politely collect the following as they become relevant — do NOT ask all at once:
- Full name
- Contact number or email
- Service they are interested in
- Preferred appointment date and time (if booking)
- Doctor preference (if any)
- Reason for visit / main concern

For example: if someone wants to book, ask for their name, contact, preferred date/time, and which doctor or service they need.

## RULES
- NEVER give specific medical diagnoses or treatment advice. Say: "I'm not a doctor, but I'd recommend booking a consultation so our physician can properly assess you."
- If asked about doctor availability, say: "Please call us at +1234567890 to confirm availability."
- Always end your reply with a helpful follow-up offer or question.
- Keep replies to 2–4 sentences unless more detail is truly needed.
- If you don't know something, say so honestly and direct them to call or email.

## FORMATTING
- Reply in plain, conversational text. NO markdown — no asterisks (**), no ## headers, no bullet lists.
- When confirming details, use a short natural sentence, e.g.
  "Got it — General Consultation for Airone Villasor (0949...) on June 21 at 9 AM. Shall I finalize it?"
- Keep it warm and brief: 2–4 sentences.


## IMPORTANT — STRUCTURED DATA OUTPUT
At the END of every single reply, always append this hidden block on a new line. The user will NEVER see it — it gets stripped before display. Use null for anything not yet known. Always reflect the LATEST known values based on the full conversation so far:

[INQUIRER_DATA]
{
  "name": "<full name or null>",
  "contact": "<phone number or email or null>",
  "intent": "<one of: appointment_booking | service_inquiry | general_inquiry | doctor_inquiry | lab_request | pharmacy | other>",
  "service_interested": "<one or more of: General Consultation, Pediatrics, Women's Health, Laboratory Services, Pharmacy, or null>",
  "appointment_date": "<preferred date as YYYY-MM-DD or null>",
  "appointment_time": "<preferred time as HH:MM or null>",
  "doctor_preference": "<doctor name or null>",
  "reason_for_visit": "<brief description of concern or null>",
  "conversation_summary": "<1-2 sentence summary of what the user needs>",
  "status": "<one of: new | in_progress | appointment_set | completed | needs_followup>"
}
[/INQUIRER_DATA]
